candidate_spans.text: Seizures: The app logs indicate a regular pattern of seizures twice every two weeks, typically clustering on days following consecutive late shifts. Episodes are described as brief events with preserved awareness followed by fatigue and mild headache; there have been no injuries, tongue biting, or incontinence reported. Partners’ observations, entered into the app on shared entries, corroborate the timing and brief duration. No rescue medication has been required. Th

gency department attendance for a seizure-related event was over a year ago. 

Examination: Alert and oriented. Speech fluent. Cranial nerves intact. No focal neurological deficits. Gait normal. Cardiovascular and respiratory examinations unremarkable. 

Investigations: 
- Routine bloods (FBC, U&E, LFTs) from August 2025 were wit

nable adjustments with occupational health. 
- Standard safety counselling reinforced (showers rather than baths, avoid heights, cook with caution). 
- Follow-up in clinic in 4 months, or sooner if there is an increase beyond the current pattern of seizures twice every two weeks or any event associated with injury or loss of awareness. 

Yours sincerely
